steps.title: Identify the appositive type
steps.content: ”Drummer” and “Dennis Chambers” form an appositive pair. Since “Dennis Chambers” is essential for identifying the drummer, it's a restrictive appositive.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Restrictive appositives use no commas. “American drummer Dennis Chambers” correctly keeps both elements together without punctuation.

Choice C uses non-restrictive punctuation (commas on both sides); choices A and D have comma placement errors that violate standard conventions.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
